briefdescription.para: This currently comprises.

simplesect.para: true if an error is catastrophic.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlError *err, void *ctxt, xmlNodePtr node, int domain, int code, xmlErrorLevel level, const char *file, int line, const char *str1, const char *str2, const char *str3, int int1, int col, const char *fmt, va_list ap)
memberdef.name: xmlVSetError

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlError *err, void *ctxt, xmlNodePtr node, int domain, int code, xmlErrorLevel level, const char *file, int line, const char *str1, const char *str2, const char *str3, int int1, int col, const char *fmt,...)
memberdef.name: xmlSetError

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlError *err, void *ctxt, xmlNodePtr node, int domain, int code, xmlErrorLevel level, const char *file, int line, const char *str1, const char *str2, const char *str3, int int1, int col, const char *fmt, va_list ap)
memberdef.name: xmlVUpdateError

briefdescription.para: Default handler for out-of-context error messages.
Set the thread-local "generic" handler and context for error messages.

detaileddescription.para: If you only want to disable parser errors being printed to stderr, use xmlParserOption XML_PARSE_NOERROR.
The generic error handler will only receive fragments of error messages which should be concatenated or printed to a stream.
If handler is NULL, use the built-in default handler which prints to stderr.

Since this is a thread-local setting, it's a good idea to reset the error handler to its default value after collecting the errors you're interested in. To get the original values, you have to access xmlGenericError and xmlGenericErrorContext directly, making this function kind of useless.
For multi-threaded applications, this must be set separately for each thread.

briefdescription.para: Set the thread-local "structured" handler and context for error messages.

detaileddescription.para: If you only want to disable parser errors being printed to stderr, use xmlParserOption XML_PARSE_NOERROR.
It's recommended to use the per-context error handlers instead:
If handler is NULL, the error handler is deactivated.
The structured error handler takes precedence over "generic" handlers, even per-context generic handlers.

Since this is a thread-local setting, it's a good idea to reset the error handler to its default value after collecting the errors you're interested in. To get the original values, you have to access xmlStructuredError and xmlStructuredErrorContext directly, making this function kind of useless.
For multi-threaded applications, this must be set separately for each thread.

briefdescription.para: Displays the associated file and line information for the current input.
Displays current context within the input content for error reporting.
Displays current context within the input content for error reporting.
Report a formatted error to a printf-like callback.

detaileddescription.para: This can result in a verbose multi-line report including additional information from the parser context.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlStructuredErrorFunc schannel, xmlGenericErrorFunc channel, void *data, int domain, xmlError *error)
memberdef.name: xmlRaiseMemoryError

briefdescription.para: Update the global and optional error structure, then forward the error to an error handler.

detaileddescription.para: This function doesn't make memory allocations which are likely to fail after an OOM error.

parameterdescription.para: optional error struct to be filled

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlStructuredErrorFunc schannel, xmlGenericErrorFunc channel, void *data, void *ctx, xmlNode *node, int domain, int code, xmlErrorLevel level, const char *file, int line, const char *str1, const char *str2, const char *str3, int int1, int col, const char *msg, va_list ap)
memberdef.name: xmlVRaiseError

briefdescription.para: Update the appropriate global or contextual error structure, then forward the error message down the parser or generic error callback handler.

parameterdescription.para: the file source of the error (or NULL)
the line of the error or 0 if N/A
column number of the error or 0 if N/A
extra parameters for the message display

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if a memory allocation failed.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlStructuredErrorFunc schannel, xmlGenericErrorFunc channel, void *data, void *ctx, xmlNode *node, int domain, int code, xmlErrorLevel level, const char *file, int line, const char *str1, const char *str2, const char *str3, int int1, int col, const char *msg,...)
memberdef.name: xmlRaiseError

briefdescription.para: Update the appropriate global or contextual error structure, then forward the error message down the parser or generic error callback handler.

parameterdescription.para: the file source of the error (or NULL)
the line of the error or 0 if N/A
column number of the error or 0 if N/A

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if a memory allocation failed.

memberdef.argsstring: (void *ctx, const char *level, const char *fmt, va_list ap)
memberdef.name: xmlVFormatLegacyError

briefdescription.para: This is the default SAX error handler, but it will never be called.

detaileddescription.para: If it isn't replaced by the user, errors will be handled by xmlFormatError.
Format an error message with additional detail from the parser context and print to generic error handler.

briefdescription.para: This is the default SAX warning handler, but it will never be called.

detaileddescription.para: If it isn't replaced by the user, warnings will be handled by xmlFormatError.
Format an warning message with additional detail from the parser context and print to generic error handler.

briefdescription.para: This is the default validity error handler, but it will never be called.

detaileddescription.para: If it isn't replaced by the user, errors will be handled by xmlFormatError.
Format an error message with additional detail from the parser context and print to generic error handler.

briefdescription.para: This is the default validity warning handler, but it will never be called.

detaileddescription.para: If it isn't replaced by the user, warnings will be handled by xmlFormatError.
Format an warning message with additional detail from the parser context and print to generic error handler.

briefdescription.para: Get the last error raised in this thread.
Reset the error to success.
Reset the last error to success.
Copy an error.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of error.
an error message for a code.

briefdescription.para: Prints to stderr.
Prints to stderr.
Print message to stderr and abort.
